items.hook: In the Night Kitchen is a children's picture book written and illustrated by Maurice Sendak, first published in hardcover in 1970 by Harper and Row.
items.body: The book depicts a young boy's dream journey through a surreal baker's kitchen where he assists in the creation of a cake to be ready by the morning. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Strokes: Reality Awaits review – a perfect marriage of mayhem and melody on their best album in years
items.body:
(RCA/Cult)
Allying the crackpot strangeness of Julian Casablancas’ side projects with his band’s gift for pop minimalism, the New Yorkers are on playful, joyous form

items.hook: Louis VII of France lays siege to Damascus during the Second Crusade.
items.body: Louis VII, was King of France from 1137 to 1180. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: ‘I know what it’s like to be vulnerable’: musician This Is Lorelei AKA Nate Amos on addiction and finding peace
items.body:
The singer-songwriter and Water from Your Eyes guitarist was unstoppably prolific, despite a growing dependence on substances. Now sober, he reflects on his new album and the songs that helped him turn his life round

items.hook: Twelve Mile Ranch is a historic ranch in the southern Black Hills of South Dakota.
items.body: It is located in Pleasant Valley, 12 miles (19 km) southwest of the city of Custer, which is where it gets its name. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.body:

items.hook: AGN-1135 is a monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) that was never marketed.
items.body: It is the racemic form of rasagiline and is a mixture of the R(+)-enantiomer and S(–)-enantiomer (TVP-1022). Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Our Fathers (1999) is the debut novel by Scottish novelist Andrew O'Hagan.
items.body: It was shortlisted for the Booker Prize (1999). The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Themistoklis Sofoulis becomes Prime Minister of Greece.
items.body: Themistoklis Sofoulis or Sophoulis was a prominent centrist and liberal Greek politician from Samos Island, who served three times as Prime Minister of Greece, with the Liberal Party, which he led for many years. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Complete Guide to Parenting is a British television comedy drama series broadcast on ITV in 2006.
items.body: Created and written by Paul Smith, the series stars Peter Davison as George Huntley, Professor of Child Psychology at London University, best-selling author of Hey Mum & Dad, Get Your Act Together and LBC resident parenting guru. The world is stranger than it

items.hook: Apollo program: Apollo 11 splashes down safely in the Pacific Ocean.
items.body: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: At the opening of the American National Exhibition in Moscow, U.S. vice president Richard Nixon and Soviet premier Nikita Khrushchev have a "Kitchen Debate".
items.body: The American National Exhibition, held from July 25 to September 4, 1959, was an exhibition of American art, fashion, cars, capitalism, model homes and futuristic kitchens. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Citizens of Leeuwarden, Netherlands, strike against a ban on foreign beer.
items.body: Leeuwarden is a city and municipality in Friesland, Netherlands, with a population of 127,073 (2023). Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Add to playlist: the ramshackle, soulful twang of Twisted Teens and the week’s best new tracks
items.body:
The New Orleans country-punk duo are on a hot streak, about to release their third album in a year, and the crunchy, literate hooks keep coming

From New Orleans
Recommended if you like Parquet C

items.hook: Battle of Nocera between Ranulf II of Alife and Roger II of Sicily.
items.body: The Battle of Nocera or Scafati was the first major battle of Roger II of Sicily and the first of his two major defeats at the hands of Count Ranulf of Alife. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘An anthem for the co-dependent’: Bad Magpie’s corvid carnage with a side of existential angst
items.body:
Going by the mantra, if you want to make someone laugh, make them cry first, the game’s developers have created a lonely magpie who fills the void by wreaking havoc
